rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use visible identifiers when available; otherwise, use valid feature names from the current scope
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations and comparisons
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architecture clarity
Use visible identifiers when available to ensure contract consistency
Avoid implementation-only paths in component type contracts to maintain type-level abstraction
Use AGREE scalar types for declarations and comparisons to ensure type safety
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators (`and`, `or`, `not(...)`, `=>`) for generated contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not use opaque Boolean data range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
